questions.prompt: Why do Falcon 9 and Super Heavy use many small engines (9 and 33 respectively) rather than a few large ones?

choices.text: Engine-out redundancy plus manufacturing scale. Many small engines mean a single engine failure isn't mission-ending — Falcon 9 has flown to orbit after losing a Merlin mid-flight (CRS-1). Small engines are also easier to develop, transport, test, and manufacture at scale than giant ones. The Soviet N1 lesson was about lacking the control systems to coordinate many engines; modern flight computers handle 33-engine clusters precisely. SpaceX explicitly chose this architecture to enable rapid iteration and serial production — they can build, test, and fly hundreds of engines per year, which would be impossible with monolithic Saturn V F-1-style engines.

questions.explanation: Many-engine clusters are deliberate. Redundancy, manufacturability, and fine-grained thrust control all favor smaller engines in larger numbers — given modern flight computers and engine-monitoring systems that can coordinate them reliably.

questions.prompt: Why did SpaceX redesign Raptor from version 1 to version 2 with explicit manufacturing-simplification goals, when Raptor 1 already worked?

choices.text: Cadence requires cost. Starship needs roughly 39 Raptors per stack (33 booster + 6 ship) and SpaceX targets high launch cadence — that's potentially hundreds of engines per year. Raptor 1 worked but was expensive and time-consuming to manufacture. Raptor 2 was redesigned for fewer parts, integrated welds, simpler plumbing — explicitly to bring engine cost down and production rate up. Performance went up at the same time because the simpler design also enabled higher chamber pressure. This 'manufacturing-driven engine design' is a recurring SpaceX pattern: design for the rate you need to fly at, not just for performance.

questions.explanation: Engine cost and cadence are limiting variables for a high-flight-rate program. Raptor 1 was 'works'; Raptor 2 is 'works AND can be made by the hundreds.' Raptor 3 pushes further on both fronts. Manufacturing is engine design.
